- World model: Level 1 has a black background with: - A green horizontal bar (rows 9-11, cols 27-32) with a red dot inside at (10,31) - A yellow vertical bar (rows 28-35, cols 9-11) with a red dot inside at (34,10) - A gray container (rows 18-24, cols 36-48) with two compartments containing light green U-shapes - A gray container (rows 35-47, cols 21-27) with two compartments containing yellow U-shapes - Two red cross patterns at (10,52) and (52,10) - A gray bar at row 63 (likely HUD/timer)
- Goal model: Consume all colored bars by clicking on matching U-shapes in containers. Each click removes a chunk (3 units) from the bar.
- Action model: Only MOUSE action available. Need to probe to understand mechanics.
- Plan: Click the green U-shape again to fully consume the green bar, then click yellow U-shapes to consume the yellow bar.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Only 1 cell changed - just the HUD. Clicking the red dot didn't do anything meaningful. Let me think more systematically about what I haven't tried yet.
So far I've tried:
1. Clicking red cross at (10, 52) - only HUD changed
2. Clicking red dot in yellow bar at (34, 10) - only HUD changed
3. Clicking left green U-shape at (21, 39) - shrunk green bar
4. Clicking right green U-shape at (21, 45) - restored green bar
5. Clicking left green U-shape again - shrunk green bar again
6. Clicking top yellow U-shape at (38, 24) - shrunk yellow bar
7. Clicking red dot in yellow bar at (31, 10) - only HUD changed
I haven't tried:
- Clicking the bottom yellow U-shape
- Clicking the bars themselves (not the red dots)
- Clicking the gray container walls
- Clicking the charcoal compartments
Let me try clicking the bottom yellow U-shape to see if it restores the yellow bar (like the right green U-shape restored the green bar). If so, the mechanic is consistent: first/left/top U-shape shrinks, second/right/bottom U-shape restores.
But I also need to think about what the actual goal is. Maybe:
1. The goal is to shrink both bars and leave them shrunk
2. The goal is to match some pattern
3. The goal involves the red crosses somehow
Let me try clicking the bottom yellow U-shape, and also try clicking on the gray bar/frames around the containers.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click the bottom yellow U-shape to test if it restores the yellow bar
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 44, 'col': 24}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
